data.title: August Dividend Kings: 3 Ideal Buys In 25 'Safer' Of 50 Dogs
data.description:
Foreword

As supplement to this article, please note that The Motley Fool, which sourced this latest list, and SureDividend both update their lists periodically. Kiplinger also featured this recent selection of Kings.

Note that two Kings failed to increase dividends last year and so departed from the list, Leggett & Platt (LEG), and 3M Co (MMM).

This August collection numbered 55 Kings when Automatic Data Processing (ADP) and RLI Corporation (RLI) joined the ranks back in February.

Indeed, most of the King collection is too pricey to justify their skinny dividends. However, three of the top-ten, by yield, live up to the author’s ideal of offering annual dividends (from a $1K investment) exceeding their single share prices. Furthermore, this month, there are 12 more to watch.

In the current market adjustment, it is now possible for six, Altria Group Inc (MO), Universal (UVV), Northwest Natural Holding Co (NWN), Canadian Utilities Ltd (OTCPK:CDUAF), United Bankshares (UBSI), and Hormel Foods Corp (HRL), inside the top ten, with one more, Kenvue (KVUE), outside the top ten, to be fair-priced. Those fair priced seven show their annual-yield (from $1,000 invested) meeting, or exceeding, their single-share prices.

The dozen more to watch are: Stanley Black & Decker (SWK); Black Hills Corp (BKH); Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM); Fortis Inc (FTS); H2O America (HTO); Stephan Co (SCL); Middlesex Water Co (MSEX); California Water Service (CWT); ABM Industries (ABM); Gorman-Rupp (GRC); H.B. Fuller (FUL); Tootsie-Roll (TR).

BKH is $14.53 away and is the closest. FTS is $15.17 from the tipping point. ADM, SWK, HTO, CWT, GRC and TR are less than $30 off the mark.

As we have now passed the five-year mark of the 2020 Ides of March dip, the time to snap-up six lingering top-yield dividend King dogs may be at hand... unless another big bearish drop in price looms ahead. (At which time your strategy would be to add to your position in any of those you then hold.) To learn which (if any) of these Kings show “safer” dividends (with sufficient cash reserves to cover their payout) drop down to the Afterword at the tail of this article. On second thought, I’ll save you the trouble, there are the three: MO; UBSI; HRL.

Actionable Conclusions (1-10): Analysts Predict 16% To 75.68% Top-Ten Kingly Net Gains For August

Five of the ten top-Kings by yield were among the top-ten gainers for the coming year based on analyst 1-year target prices. (They are tinted gray in the chart below). Thus, this yield-based August 6 forecast (as graded by Brokers) was 50% accurate.

Estimated dividend returns from $1000 invested in each of these highest-yielding stocks and their aggregate one-year analyst median target-prices, as reported by YCharts, produced the following 2025-26 data points. (Note: target-prices from lone-analysts were not used.) Ten probable profit-generating trades projected to August, 2026 were:

Stephan was projected to net $756.80, based on dividends, plus the median of target price estimates from 2 analysts, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 2% less than the market as a whole.

Northwest Natural Holding was projected to net $310.74, based on dividends, plus the median of target price estimates from 4 analysts, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 45% less than the market as a whole.

Stanley Black & Decker was projected to net $305.66 based on dividends, plus the median of target price estimates from 15 analysts, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 16% over the market as a whole.

H2O America was projected to net $254.47, based on a median of target estimates from 4 analysts, plus dividends, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 47% less than the market as a whole.

Middlesex Water netted $253.73 based on a median target price estimate from 3 analysts, plus dividends, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 19% less than the market as a whole.

Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) was projected to net $225.24, based on the median of target price estimates from 18 analysts, plus the estimated annual dividend, less broker fees. The Beta number showed 14% greater than the market as a whole.

PPG Industries (PPG) was projected to net $222.40, based on the median of target price estimates from 20 analysts, plus dividends, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 17% more than the market as a whole.

California Water Service Group was projected to net $218.11 based on target price estimates from 5 analysts, plus annual dividend, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate is subject to risk/volatility 34% less than the market as a whole.

Hormel Foods was projected to net $177.54, based on dividends, plus the median of target price estimates from 8 analysts, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate is subject to risk/volatility 22% less than the market as a whole.

United Bankshares was projected to net $160.13, based on the median of target price estimates from 6 analysts, plus dividends, less broker fees. The Beta number showed this estimate subject to risk/volatility 21% under the market as a whole.

The average net gain in dividend and price was estimated to be 28.85% on $10k invested as $1k in each of these ten stocks. The average Beta ranking showed these estimates subject to risk/volatility 14% under the market as a whole.

The Dividend Dogs Rule

Stocks earned the "dog" moniker by exhibiting three traits: (1) paying reliable, repeating dividends, (2) their prices fell to where (3) yield (dividend/price) grew higher than their peers. Thus, the highest yielding stocks in any collection became known as "dogs." More precisely, these are, in fact, best called, "underdogs", even if they are "Kings."

47 Dividend Kings By Broker Targets

This scale of broker-estimated upside (or downside) for stock prices provides a measure of market popularity. Note: no broker coverage or single broker coverage produced a zero score on the above scale. Negative broker ratings were ignored. These broker estimates can be seen as the emotional component (as opposed to the strictly monetary and objective dividend/price yield-driven report below). As noted above, these scores may also be regarded as contrarian.

55 Dividend Kings By Yield

Actionable Conclusions (11-20): Ten Top Stocks By Yield Are The August Dogs Of The Dividend Kings

Top ten King Dogs selected 8/6/25 by yield represented five of eleven Morningstar sectors.

The first of four consumer staples representatives took first place, Altria Group Inc. [1], another placed second, Universal Corp [2], while one placed eighth, Target (TGT) [8], and the last was tenth, Hormel Foods (HRL) [10].

Then three utilities placed third, fifth, and seventh: Northwest Natural Holding Co [3], Canadian Utilities Ltd [5], and Black Hills Corp [7].

Then fourth place was claimed by the industrials representative, Stanley Black & Decker [4].

Sixth place was claimed by a lone real estate representative, Federal Realty Investment Trust [6].

Finally, a lone financials member placed ninth, United Bankshares [9], to complete these August top-ten Kings, by yield.

Dividend Vs. Price Results

The graph above shows relative strengths of the top ten August Kings by dividend and price as of market close 8/6/2025.

This month four of the top-ten Kings by yield show a less than ideal status because the prices of those four exceed the projected annual dividends from $1k invested in each.

As noted above, a dividend dogcatcher IDEAL priority is to select stocks whose dividends from $1K invested are greater than their single share price. That condition was reached by six of the highest yield Kings.

Altria, Universal, Northwest Natural, Canadian Utilities, United Bankshares, and Hormel, in the top ten, while Kenvue, outside the top ten, made the IDEAL. All seven lived up to the dogcatcher ideal of annual dividends from $1K invested exceeding single share prices.

Further, Stanley Black & Decker; Black Hills Corp; Archer-Daniels-Midland; Fortis Inc; H2O America; Stephan Co; Middlesex Water Co; California Water Service; ABM Industries (ABM); Gorman-Rupp ; H.B. Fuller; Tootsie-Roll, all showed prices within $35 of meeting that ideal goal.

55 Dividend King Costs per Dividend Dollar as of 8/6/25

The above chart reveals 55 King Dividend dogs subjected to the same test: divide the price of each stock by the annual dividend. Thus is calculated the cost per dividend dollar for each stock.

The least expensive dog of the August Kings is Altria at $15.48 per dividend dollar and the most expensive is S&P Global at $146.50 per dividend dollar.

Actionable Conclusion (21): Nineteen of Fifty-Five Kings Show Neutral or Negative Free Cash Flow Margins

A negative gap between dividend yield and free cash flow yield defines a cash-poor stock. That is, their dividend payout exceeds their cash on hand to pay dividends. Nineteen dividend Kings so infected are: Northwest Natural, Stanley Black & Decker, Canadian Utilities, Federal Realty, Black Hills, PepsiCo, Kenvue, Fortis, H2O America, Consolidated Edison, Stephan, Genuine Parts, Coca-Cola, American States Water, Middlesex Water, California Water, National Fuel Gas, ABM Industries, and Nucor. These are considered unsafe to buy.

Remember this dogcatcher yield-based stock-picking strategy is contrarian. That means rooting for (buying) the underdog is productive when you don't already own those stocks. If you do hold those stocks, then you must look for opportune pull-backs in price to add to your position to best improve your dividend yield. Plenty of pull-back opportunities appear to be ahead. Dread the Fed, deportation, uncertainty, deals, wars, and tariffs, too!

Price Drops or Dividend Increases Could Get All Ten Top-Yield Kings Back to "Fair Price" Rates For Investors

The charts above retain the current dividend amount and adjust share price to produce a yield (from $1K invested) to equal or exceed the single share price of each stock. As the top illustration shows, six in the top ten are already ideally priced. Beside those six, already in the ideal zone, four more top-yield stocks are within $36 of getting there.

The alternative, of course, could be that these companies raise their dividends or declares stock splits to achieve fair pricing. That may be too much to ask in these highly disrupted, inflationary, recessionary, uncertain, yet cash-rich, and volatile times. Mr. Market is quite effective at moving prices up or down to appropriate amounts, just watch and buy when your targeted stock price moves into a sweet spot.

Actionable Conclusions: (21-31) Top Ten Kings Showed 13.36% To 72.55% Upsides Into August 2026; (31) Five Downsiders Lagged -2.27% to -40.20%

To quantify top-yield rankings, analyst median price-target estimates made a "market sentiment" gauge of upside potential. Added to the simple high-yield metrics, analyst median price-target-estimates became another tool to dig-out bargains.

Analysts Estimated A 3.36% Advantage For 5 Highest Yield, Lowest Priced, of Top-Ten Dividend Kings By August, 2026

Ten top Kings were culled by yield for their monthly update. Yield (dividend/price) results verified by YCharts did the ranking.

As noted above, top ten Kings selected 8/6/25 showing the highest dividend yields represented five of eleven Morningstar sectors.

Actionable Conclusions: Analysts Estimated 5 Lowest-Priced Of Ten Highest-Yield Dividend Kings (32) Delivering 14.72% Vs. (33) 14.24% Net Gains by All Ten by August, 2026

$5000 invested as $1k in each of the five lowest-priced stocks in the top ten Dividend Kings kennel by yield were predicted by analyst 1-year targets to deliver 3.36% more gain than $5,000 invested as $.5k in all ten. The fourth lowest-priced top-yield King stock, Northwest Natural Holding, was projected to deliver the best net gain of 31.07%.

The five lowest-priced top-yield Dividend Kings as of August 6 were: Canadian Utilities; Hormel Foods; United Bankshares; Northwest Natural Holding; Universal, with prices ranging from $28.24 to $54.52

Five higher-priced top-yield Dividend Kings by the August 6 accounting were: Black Hills; Altria; Stanley Black & Decker; Federal Realty; Target, whose prices ranged from $59.77 to $105.39.

This distinction between five low-priced dividend dogs and the general field of ten reflected Michael B. O'Higgins' "basic method" for beating the Dow. The scale of projected gains based on analyst targets added a unique element of "market sentiment" gauging upside potential. It provided a here-and-now equivalent of waiting a year to find out what might happen in the market. Caution is advised, however, since analysts are historically only 15% to 85% accurate on the direction of change and just 0% to 15% accurate on the degree of change.

Afterword

25 ‘Safer' August Dividend Kings

Early August, 2025, 25 of the 55 Dividend Kings were tagged "safer" for dividends as they showed positive one-year returns and free-cash-flow-yields greater-than dividend-yields. Furthermore, YCharts 8/6/25 data showed three were fair-priced.

Dividend King ‘Safer’ Dividend Vs. Price Data Show “Fair Price” Results For Investors

The chart at the top above reveals three dividends from $1K invested in our top ten 'safer' dividend Kings conform to the dogcatcher standard of being equal to (or greater than) the price of a single share. They are: Altria; United Bankshares; Hormel.

The middle chart shows the recent dividend amount and the bottom chart adjusts that share price to produce a yield (from $1K invested) equal to or greater than the single share price of each stock; a so-called, “fair price.”

A market correction of 65.27% could put all ten share prices of these “safer” survivors above or equal to their dividend payouts from $1k invested.

There are, of course, other ways for dividends from $1K invested to exceed the price of a single share of stock. That would be for the boards of directors these companies to raise dividends to the dogcatcher ideal level. to Split shares. That’s a lot to ask with all the corporate funds doing so much “good” elsewhere.

Stay tuned for future Dividend Dogcatcher King updates, out here, in public, away from the pay-walled Seeking Alpha Investing Group site (formerly known as the Marketplace).

Footnotes

A Descending List of Dividend King Longevity

The net gain estimates mentioned above did not factor-in any foreign or domestic tax problems resulting from distributions. Consult your tax advisor regarding the source and consequences of "dividends" from any investment.

Stocks listed above were suggested only as possible starting points for your "Safer" Kingly dividend stock research process. These were not recommendations.

Disclaimer: This article is for informational and educational purposes only and should not be construed to constitute investment advice. Nothing contained herein shall constitute a solicitation, recommendation or endorsement to buy or sell any security. Prices and returns on equities in this article except as noted are listed without consideration of fees, commissions, taxes, penalties, or interest payable due to purchasing, holding, or selling same.

Graphs and charts were compiled by Rydlun & Co., LLC from data derived from YCharts; finance.yahoo.com; analyst mean target price by YCharts. Open source dog art from dividenddogcatcher.com.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Advantage Energy: Strategic Alternatives Review Announced
data.description:
Advantage Energy (OTCPK:AAVVF) announced a strategic alternatives review was formed by the board of directors. Until this completes it likely overshadows just about anything else in the quarterly review. Evidently all pathways are to be considered at this time. But there is no guarantee that anything will come out of it. The Charlie Lake acquisition has added material value to the company's operations. Now whether this company should combine with other companies or be acquired will be up to the board to consider. The stock price may be somewhat "hesitant" until such an important process completes.

Results Summary

The additional liquids that have resulted from the Charlie Lake drilling activity during the period of largely weak natural gas prices that ended with the first quarter (thanks to the cold winter) has added materially to cash flow.

(Note: Advantage Energy is a Canadian company that reports using Canadian dollars unless otherwise noted.)

What makes the addition of more liquids production so valuable is that the average price per barrel went up considerably but the costs to produce that sale remained virtually the same. Liquids are a very different business. So the costs per barrel shown above could have been very different. It is very likely this company found an extremely profitable interval. The result was a deluge of cash flow from just a little bit more production as shown above.

Crude oil sales are now roughly one-third of sales dollars despite being roughly 10% of total production. It turns out that the one-third of sales that are crude oil sales are tremendously profitable for the company.

This enabled funds flow to take a big jump over the previous fiscal year despite the weakening commodity prices experienced throughout the industry. Essentially the company now has a comparatively more favorable production mix than it has had in the past.

While operating expenses (shown above) did climb, those climbing expenses were a small enough change that most of the benefits of oil production showed as increasing cash flow while costs essentially remained at natural gas levels. This is a "game" that many natural gas producers attempt to play to increase profitability when natural gas is just not all that profitable to produce. Here, it is clearly working.

The company has roughly C$700 million of debt. Of that debt roughly C$148 million is Entropy financing that is nonrecourse to Advantage Energy. That leaves about C$570 million of debt to Advantage Energy. But the company has already generated nearly C$200 million in adjusted funds flow and repaid about C$50 million in debt in the current fiscal year.

Those are impressive numbers. The change over the previous fiscal year due to the results of one acquisition has made all the difference in the world to this formerly dry gas producer that had been slowly transitioning to profitable liquids production. While increasing North American natural gas exports will help the natural gas situation; the company is no longer dependent upon the natural gas outlook.

Charlie Lake Progress

The company has owned this acquisition for about one year. The drilling and activity here was meant to offset some of the risks of the natural gas outlook uncertainty. But the profitability has so far exceeded expectations.

Management has essentially kept the original message the same for this slide while bringing on additional wells. There will likely be more information available on recent results possibly by the next corporate presentation update (but definitely by the next quarterly report).

Since the message has been kept the same, there are likely no significant deviations from what is shown above based upon newer preliminary results.

The extra liquids in the production mix enhance profitability with only a few higher deductions along the way. That means that management does not have to spend the capital that it otherwise would have to achieve budgeted sales and cash flow results. Of course, the capital could be spent anyway which would likely result in more free cash flow and higher profits.

While many producers I cover are reporting the effects of weakening commodity prices, here the transition to more oil production overwhelms that story. It is something unique to smaller producers as a much larger company needs far more than a few thousand barrels of something favorable to make a material difference.

This company has additional acreage that has a lot of liquids production. It may be possible to transfer what is being learned here to some other acreage which will help the profitability elsewhere.

The big deal here is that free cash flow was going to be negative without the acquisition. That has of course changed materially which also changes the outlook of the company for at least a few years.

It might be an idea for the company to pursue a "rollup strategy" in the Lake Charlie interval to see if more of this acreage can be added to the company inventory.

Summary

There are different ways for commodity companies to deal with a cyclical downturn. This company made a very good acquisition that has materially changed the future of the company.

I follow a lot of companies that can "turn a toad into a prince" while the corporate backyard is loading up with toads over time. This company has actually found a real gem in the process of lowering operating costs on the acquired acreage. Actually, getting what was promised before the acquisition is probably less common than realized.

But this is what shareholders pay management to do. The future of this company no longer solely depends upon natural gas as a result. This is also an area with multiple intervals. Some like the Charlie Lake interval have only recently become cost competitive. But there are more there that could become productive as long as technology keeps advancing.

The other thing to realize is that a lot of the new intervals have recovery rates in the single digits. But that leaves more than 90% of the oil present to be recovered in the future (again as technology advances). We are not and never have come close to running out of oil and gas. However, the cost to produce it is another matter entirely.

Advantage Energy remains a strong buy consideration as this rather small company has proved to be amazingly flexible in avoiding a loss period. I would bet on management to provide superior returns well into the future. Companies like this that engage in strategic alternatives are well worth watching as I value their opinions as a result of the strategy chosen.

Risks

This is a company demonstrating the benefits of technology advances in migrating to more liquids production. But those technology advances can stop at any time or happen years after they are badly needed.

A management that can avoid a cyclical downturn as was done here is a fairly rare management (even if they cannot do it again). A loss of services of this type of management could prove crucial to the future of the company. Such people are generally hard to replace with someone equally effective.

The current political uncertainty brought about by bargaining and position changes appear to be bringing on a recession. If it continues much longer, the results could be worse. Therefore, a replacement of the current situation with permanent (constructive) agreements and far more certainty is essential.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Sun Life Financial: Sell-Off Creates Attractive Entry Point
data.description:
Introduction

Sun Life (NYSE:SLF) (TSX:SLF:CA) is one of Canada's largest financial services companies. Sun Life has well over C$1T in assets under management. And with a strong presence in Canada, the United States, and Asia, the company has a robust history of paying dividends, making it an interesting idea for income-oriented investors. Sun Life reported on its second quarter last week, and given the recent volatility in the financial markets, it's perhaps not a surprise to see the share price decrease by a high single-digit percentage, even though the Q2 results were pretty much in line with expectations.

A major difference between reported earnings and underlying earnings

One of the main issues Sun Life has to deal with is its sometimes rather large discrepancy between the reported results and the underlying results. While it's always easy to just also announce the underlying results and pretend the non-recurring or occasional elements can be ignored, I'd like to have a look at both, considering the reported earnings can sometimes limit a company’s ability to pay out its shareholders.

In the second quarter of 2025, Sun Life reported a net insurance service result of C$825M, which is a 2% increase compared to the same quarter a year ago. And as the income statement below shows, the total net investment result increased by 1% as well, while the fee income jumped by roughly 3%.

Those are all good evolutions, but keep in mind the total amount of expenses increased as well, although the interest expenses showed a single-digit decrease. Taking all these elements into consideration, the total pre-tax income increased by approximately 7% to C$976M, resulting in a net profit of roughly C$778M, of which C$735M was attributable to Sun Life.

From that amount, we still have to deduct the C$19M in preferred dividends, which means the total net profit attributable to the company’s common shareholders was approximately C$716M, representing an earnings per share of C$1.29. As the income statement shows, this brings the total EPS in the first half of the year to C$2.89.

The income statement also very clearly shows that the preferred dividends enjoy an exceptionally strong coverage ratio, as the insurance and asset management company needed less than 3% of its net profit to cover these preferred dividends. It also goes without saying that the current quarterly dividend of C$0.88 on the common share is very well covered. Sun Life’s dividend policy is centered around a payout ratio of 40% to 50% of the underlying net profit. And considering the consensus estimates are calling for that underlying EPS to increase above C$8 per share from FY 2027 on, there seems to be a clear path forward for a dividend increase towards C$1 per quarter further down the road.

As shown below, the underlying results exclude the impact of the interest rate fluctuations and the fair value of the investment properties, while they also exclude the restructuring and integration expenses and the amortization expenses related to the intangible assets.

That's why it makes sense to have a look at the company from an underlying earnings perspective, considering a substantial portion of these adjustments are non-cash items.

A closer look at some preferred dividends

While the dividend yield on the common shares is already attractive, especially considering there is a clear path forward to seeing continuous increases, investors who want an additional layer of security could perhaps also have a look at the preferred shares issued by Sun Life. There are only a handful, and their specific terms are summarized in the table below.

The Series 3 and 4 are currently trading at around C$21.4-21.8 per preferred share, which means the 4.45% preferred dividend yield based on the C$25 principal value per share implies a 5.2% preferred dividend yield.

I'm also keeping an eye on the preferred shares Series 8R, which recently reset the dividend, locking in a 4.23% preferred dividend yield based on the five-year Canada government bond yield plus a 141 bps markup. This specific series of preferred shares is trading at just C$18.73, which indicates the current yield is approximately 5.65%. Of course, it's impossible to tell what the five-year Canadian government bond yield will be in five years from now when the preferred dividend will be reset again, but this 5.65% yield for the next five years could be attractive to some of the dividend investors.

Investment thesis

I think that in Sun Life's case, the "hybrid" approach could work. After the recent share price decrease, the common shares currently offer a dividend yield of approximately 4.5% with a payout ratio of approximately 70% based on the reported earnings and about 50% of the underlying earnings. There's plenty of room for growth for the common dividends. Additionally, owning some of the preferred shares could really boost the dividend income, and I think the Series 8R, which were just reset, offer an attractive risk/reward ratio, although all preferred shares issued by Sun Life are non-cumulative in nature.

I currently have no exposure to Sun Life, but I think the recent share price decrease on the back of the Q2 results provides an interesting entry point in the common shares, while I'm also closely watching the preferred stock.

data.title: Palantir: Don't Wait Till The Bubble Implodes
data.description:
Palantir: Extreme Valuation Doesn't Matter?

How do we make sense of a software stock that's trading at a forward earnings multiple of almost 245x, as compared to the software median of 41x? I don't think I need to provide any reminder, just which high-powered software company I'm referring to: Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:PLTR). The company has scored another momentum milestone in PLTR's Q2 earnings release earlier this week, achieving the $1B quarterly revenue milestone, while "defanging" the naysayers (Let me guess? Me?).

AI stocks have become the big winners in the tech-led advance that has also boosted the valuations of the Magnificent Seven close to the 30x forward earnings multiple level. Yet, that still doesn't come quite close to the Pollyannaish investors who continue to bang the table about the bullish thesis of PLTR as it surged to another new high this week.

In my previous PLTR write-up, I downgraded the stock as I assessed that the valuations on the leading AI platform company no longer make much sense to me. Yet, I'm also reminded that being excessively overvalued doesn't necessarily portend the (immediate) collapse of a highly regarded stock that has continued to execute robustly. CEO Alex Karp has flipped the pessimistic script while also hammering home his "disdain" for analysts/investors who fail to buy into his conviction. And, could anyone really blame him for that, while Wall Street has upgraded its near-term estimates on Palantir, they haven't really followed through with their price target?

Palantir: "Defang" The Pessimists

As seen above, PLTR's magnificent rally has compelled analysts to keep raising their average price targets over time, yet they are still struggling to keep pace with it. Palantir's bullish proposition has always been predicated on its topology systems layer, which the company considers a pivotal operating system to operationalize the current AI stack effectively.

And if you consider just how quickly Palantir's commercial business has accelerated over the past 12 months, it becomes very clear that the validation from enterprise customers has also spurred referral opportunities, contributing to the growth of Palantir's multi-year commercial backlog.

As a reminder, Palantir's total revenue grew by 48% YoY, outperforming Wall Street's estimates. Notably, US commercial business has become an outstanding growth pillar for the company, delivering a 93% growth rate in the past quarter. As a result, it has been instrumental in driving Palantir's corporate commercial base, which increased by 35% over the past 12 months. Therefore, I believe the market has likely felt vindicated by the acceleration in its US enterprise base as it attempts to justify its stratospheric valuation.

What's also clear is that the multi-year trajectory has also been significantly augmented. Note that Palantir reported a US remaining deal value of $2.79B (up 145% YoY), representing a 20% sequential growth. And with a total RDV of $7.1B, Palantir sees a massive opportunity to chart a multi-year trajectory of robust growth ahead.

What If Palantir Hits Peak Growth This Year?

Yet, even with the upgraded Q3 revenue guidance that could see Palantir post a 50% YoY growth, analysts remain skeptical about whether the growth momentum can be maintained through 2026. Note that Palantir's defense business has consistently been the most crucial underpinning for the company before its commercial business gained credence, bolstered by the success of its AIP boot camps. And Palantir's government business also appears to be growing from strength to strength under Karp's incisive leadership, as validated by the recent $10B multi-year enterprise contract win with the US Army.

Although Palantir's government segment still accounted for 55% of its overall revenue base in Q2, the visibility gleaned from the US commercial RDV has also unveiled a relatively clear pathway if the company manages to execute accordingly. In other words, I believe Palantir has clearly demonstrated the significant value accorded to its AI platform, staking the company's claim as the premier AI operating system in today's increasingly complex AI business environment. And with Palantir proving that its customers could deploy its system cost-effectively while scaling quickly (cutting down deployment time), it should help deepen its engagement with pilot customers, transitioning them from experimentation to implementation.

Yet, back to the most divisive metric that bears have persevered with, while the bulls ask, what's all the hullabaloo about? The cacophony of rumbling sounds from the bears doesn't seem to worry (much less perturb) the highly convinced bulls about Palantir's ability to surpass the less enthusiastic Wall Street.

Buy or Sell PLTR Stock?

But, with a forward P/E multiple that isn't just frothy but also looking dangerously perched atop the "never-ending" rally (really?), optimistic prognosticators are arguably betting that someone out there is willing to pay even more to take those shares off them.

Look at that ride over the past two years, isn't that astounding? Not only have past pullbacks been relatively shallow, they have not stymied bulls from loading up and doubling down on those dips, and then (arguably) hoping that Karp and his team deliver the goods quarter after quarter.

Don't get me wrong. I believe Palantir's operating performance and key metrics clearly validate the success of its commercial and government business. But, with the forward multiple having nearly reached the 250x mark, don't you think that when the chickens come home to roost, the pain might also be as equally "intense" as the excitement that was felt on the way up?

To say that there's no room for disappointment is an understatement. A quarter or two of missed execution due to elongated sales cycle risks, possible contract cancellations (note that backlog doesn't guarantee revenue conversion), or simply the market rotating away from frothy growth plays, PLTR shareholders who still (ignore extreme valuation risks), and hop onto the bandwagon asking for a lot more upside, must be prepared for a subsequent market reckoning.

Rating: Maintain Sell.

data.title: Eli Lilly: Another Not-To-Miss Dip
data.description:
A look at how yesterday's trading session went for the weight loss treatments provider Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) was instructive in understanding investor psychology. Despite a robust Q2 2025 results and upgraded guidance, the stock plunged, presumably on less than desirable trial results for its oral GLP-1 treatment Orforglipron.

Here I argue why sentiment around the new drug trial has completely overwhelmed what's still an exceptionally strong stock story based on three reasons. This makes the latest drop a not-to-miss opportunity for investors, right after another version of the same happened for Eli Lilly's chief competition Novo Nordisk (NVO) late last month. But before anything else, a look at the trial results.

What the Orforglipron trial results say

The highlight of the trial results is that participants on the highest dosage of the pill lost ~0.7 kg per month on average over some 18 months. The gap of 6x between the smallest and biggest dose results in a 55% bigger weight loss for the big dose (see slide below).

While the 7.8-12.4% weight loss, depending on the dosage, met the primary endpoint of being more effective than a placebo, the treatment was less effective than Eli Lilly's other treatment Zepbound's 20.2% over the same time period. In fact, Zepbound is superior even to Novo Nordisk's (NVO) Wegovy, which showed 13.7% weight loss over 72 weeks.

Incidentally, Novo's trial treatment for weight management and diabetes, CagriSema, has the best results at 68 weeks of all the three treatments, at 22.7%. But the stock wasn't spared of a similar plunge for the Novo stock on the release of its trial results either, as its effectiveness turned out to be lower than management guidance of 25% weight loss. So, it's not surprising at all that Eli Lilly has seen a big drop.

3 reasons why Eli Lilly still looks good

As much as it's in line with past investor behaviour, though, it does look like a complete overreaction, going by the overall Eli Lilly story. From its position in the obesity market to its latest financials and guidance, the company has a lot going for it. Three factors are discussed below:

#1. Mounjaro, Zepbound and the case for Orforglipron

Along with Novo Nordisk, Eli Lilly has a solid position in the diabetes and obesity market with or without a new treatment pipeline. While CagriSema, which is due to be in the markets by early 2026, could pose competition, that's a wait and watch situation.

For now, both Mounjaro and Zepbound are making solid progress, with an 85% YoY and 3.23x increase in revenues in Q2 2025 respectively, establishing their positions in the market further. Moreover, with both treatments' patent expiration is over a decade away right now, the company has one less worry.

Also, Eli Lilly, in its earnings call, it has mentions a number of other reasons that make a case for Orforglipron, as below:

  • The company mentions simplicity in the treatment's consumption as one advantage. The pill, as opposed to other treatments that are injectables, needs to be taken "once a day without regard to food and water".
  • Besides weight loss, it can also offer the benefit of improving biomarkers like blood pressure and fasting glucose. It's also being tested for other health challenges like diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea and knee pain associated with osteoarthritis.
  • Eli Lilly also mentions that Orforglipron can be manufactured at scale. While this is only mentioned in passing, it could be a critical advantage for the company, which has struggled with meeting demand for its treatments in the recent past.

#2. Results are robust

The company's overall results for Q2 2025 are robust, indicating that it continues to stay in fine shape. Eli Lilly saw a 38% YoY increase in revenues during the quarter, bringing the H1 2025 increase to 41% YoY. What I find even more notable is its sustained high operating margin, after the company cut prices last year on receiving criticism. The non-GAAP operating margin was at a strong 44.1% during the quarter, with the non-GAAP net income margin at 36.5%. Earnings also showed solid growth, with the non-GAAP EPS increasing by 61% YoY.

#3. Upgraded guidance and smaller P/E

Next, after reducing its earnings guidance on the release of the Q1 2025 results, the company upgraded them again. The non-GAAP earnings estimate is now expected to come in in the range of USD 21.75-23, a 3.9% increase from the previous guidance. The midpoint of the latest guidance is still ~3.8% lower than the midpoint of the initial guidance range of USD 22.5-24, but the extent of drop is now half of what it was after the first revision.

At the midpoint of the latest guidance range, LLY's forward non-GAAP price-to-earnings (P/E) is now at 28.49x. This is far smaller than the 37.9x level the last time I checked and the difference from the stock's five-year average of 43.58x is even more stark. If the stock were to go back up to this average, it has an over 50% upside.

What next?

But can LLY actually go back up that much again? To look more into this aspect, I considered three arguments for why or why not:

  • How LLY fares against NVO: Its rival Novo Nordisk is trading at a far smaller ratio of 12.8x, which can make it far more attractive than LLY now. It does need to be taken into account though, that NVO can lose some share price premium on challenges in the big US market as a Danish company at a time of foreign trade and investment policy shifts. Also, historically, NVO has traded at a discount to LLY, with an average five-year non-GAAP forward P/E at 30.89x.
  • Comparison with the healthcare sector: LLY also trades at a bigger P/E compared with the S&P 500 Healthcare Index, which is presently at 16x. But then again, not all healthcare stocks are made equal. And LLY has a significant case for a premium, as a heavyweight in a big market.
  • The long-term case: Even if the stock were to see a time of turbulence for now, the fact remains that long-term projections still look very good. For 2030, its forward P/E falls to just 12.3x as per analysts' estimates on Seeking Alpha, which is glaringly low compared with the past five-years' average.

There can be risks ahead over time for LLY if it fails to develop competitive weight loss treatments. But for now, it has multiple advantages like established medications with expiration dates nowhere near, strong results and attractive market multiples. I'm retaining a Strong Buy on LLY.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: AMC, Cisco Set To Report Earnings As Investors Watch Out For Core CPI
data.description:
Get ahead of the market by subscribing to Seeking Alpha's Wall Street Week Ahead, a preview of key events scheduled for the coming week. The newsletter keeps you informed of the biggest stories set to make headlines, including upcoming IPOs, investor days, earnings reports, and conference presentations.

Wall Street ticked up on Friday, amid a rally in tech stocks that boosted market sentiment, even as investors continued to weigh the impact of Donald Trump's tariffs. Major averages on Thursday closed mixed, as positive sentiment sparked by Trump's chip tariff exemptions and progress on Russia-Ukraine de-escalation petered out. In other news, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller is reportedly gaining momentum in the process to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the central bank.

Trump's reciprocal tariffs took effect at midnight on Thursday, and traders will keep a watch on the impact of trade policies going ahead. Important economic reports due to be announced next week include reports on Core CPI and NFIB Small Business Optimism on Tuesday, with MBA Purchase Index and Crude Oil Imports on Wednesday. Thursday will see the release of Continuing Jobless Claims and Core PPI, while reports on Core Retail Sales, Industrial Production, and Business Inventories are due to be released on Friday.

AMC Entertainment (AMC), Cisco (CSCO), Applied Materials (AMAT), CoreWeave (CRWV), eTORO (ETOR), Paysafe (PSFE), Barrick Mining (B), Oklo (OKLO), Plug Power (PLUG), and Flowers Foods (FLO) will report earnings next week.

_____________________________________________________________________

Earnings spotlight: Monday, August 11: AMC Entertainment, Barrick Mining (B), Oklo (OKLO), and Plug Power (PLUG). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Tuesday, August 12: CoreWeave (CRWV), eTORO (ETOR), Paysafe (PSFE). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Wednesday, August 13: Cisco (CSCO), and Alvotech (ALVO). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Thursday, August 14: Alibaba (BABA), Applied Materials (AMAT). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Friday, August 15: Flowers Foods (FLO) and So-Young International (SY). See the full earnings calendar.

Volatility watch: aTyr Pharma (ATYR) and Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Celcuity (CELC), Jyong Biotech (MENS), and Star Fashion Culture (STFS). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Primo Brands (PRMB), Evolus (EOLS), and Fortinet (FTNT).

Dividend watch: Companies that have an ex-dividend next week include Apple (AAPL), KKR (KKR), Ford Motor Company (F), Visa (V), FTAI Aviation (FTAI), Target (TGT), Kenvue (KVUE), Paccar (PCAR), Walmart (WMT), Eli Lilly (LLY), Exxon Mobil (XOM), and Starbucks (SBUX). Read through some dividend stock picks from Seeking Alpha analysts.

IPO Watch: Bullish (BLSH) is expected to price its IPO and begin trading. The Peter Thiel-backed cryptocurrency exchange stock is looking to raise $629 million at a valuation level of over $4.2 billion. The quiet period expires during the week on Aura Minerals (AUGO), Robot Consulting (LAWR), Mega Fortune (MGRT), and K-Tech Solutions (KMRK) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Sailpoint (NASDAQ:SAIL) and Karman Holdings (KRMN).

AMC Earnings Preview: AMC Entertainment is expected to post second quarter results next week. Wall Street expects the Leawood, Kansas-based theater chain to post EPS of -$0.08 on revenue of $1.34 billion, implying a rise of over 30% during the quarter. Analysts are positive on a recovery, thanks to a consistent release slate over the coming quarters. Industry ticket sales in the U.S. soared 37%, according to data from Box Office Mojo, fueled by the success of movies including A Minecraft Movie, Thunderbolts, Sinners, and Disney's Lilo & Stitch.

Cisco Earnings Preview: Cisco is expected to report fourth quarter earnings of $0.98 per share on $14.63B revenue. The company has consistently surpassed Wall Street expectations. Upside has been driven by a cyclical recovery in core enterprise networking business and a compelling narrative around cloud and AI markets. Headwinds include challenging order growth comps and continued underperformance in Security and Observability segments. Given the CFO transition, the probability increases that we get a more conservative guide versus expected for fiscal year 2026.

Editor's Note: This article covers one or more microcap stocks. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Yield Hunting Part 32: High-Yield Attractive Opportunity From Oxford Square Capital
data.description:
Co-authored by Strategic Yield.

We continue the yield-hunting series with another one of our favorite baby bonds with high yields and low duration. The following lines are for Oxford Square Capital Corp. (OXSQ) and its baby bonds.

Company Overview

Oxford Square Capital Corp. was founded in 2003, and is headquartered in Greenwich, Connecticut. At the end of 31 March 2025, the total assets of the company were around $285.5 million, distributed in close to 58 holdings. The current market capitalization of the company is about $177 million. Below you can see the investment portfolio overview of OXSQ, divided by asset type and by industry:

The biggest parts: 29.5% of the portfolio is in the Software industry, and 29.1% in the Business Services industry.

The total leverage of OXSQ is approximately 43%, and the gross asset non-leverage expense ratio is 2.94%. It has a 1-year negative z-score of -0.91, as shown in the picture above.

And here is the 10-year historical premium/discount chart of the company:

The 5-year total return on NAV is 3.71%:

The common stock is trading at a price of $2.31 at the moment, with a premium of 10.53%:

Baby Bonds

OXSQ has two baby bonds (NASDAQ:OXSQG) and (NASDAQ:OXSQZ):

Neither is rated by the credit agencies like Moody's, S&P, and Fitch.

OXSQZ is priced at $25.00 at the moment. Its yield to worst is 6.47%. It's callable anytime; its call date was 4/30/2022. The bond has less than a year to maturity, with a maturity date of 04/30/2026 and a yield to maturity of 6.47%. It pays 6.25% annual interest, or $1.56 per annum. OXSQ issued 1.7 million shares with a $25 par value each of OXSQZ on 03/26/2019.

OXSQG is trading at $23.90 current price. Its yield to worst is 7.40%. Its call date was on 5/31/2024, so it is callable anytime. The yield to maturity is 7.40%, with close to 3 years to maturity and a maturity date of 7/31/2028. It pays 5.50% annual interest in the form of $0.34375 per quarter and $1.375 per annum. OXSQ issued 2.8 million shares with a $25 par value each of OXSQG on 05/13/2021.

After examining the metrics of both bonds, we can conclude that our preferred bond is the OXSQG, with a low duration, a higher yield of close to 8% (close to the highest in the sector), and a price below par. Let's look at both the pros and cons to make sure the company is suitable for investing in its bonds.

Strengths And Weaknesses

This aspect is important if we plan to invest in the company and its financial instruments.

As we mentioned earlier, the company has a leverage of around 43%. This provides it with an asset coverage ratio cushion of around 54%, and the total asset coverage ratio is around 230%. Compared to other companies in the sector, this is a relatively stable and average position. Over the past three years, its return on assets has been around 3.8%. Of its entire loan portfolio, around 0.2% of loans are nonaccrual, which is one of the worst scores in this metric in the sector. In addition, close to 53% of its assets are secured by senior assets, but the company doesn't have any secured debt, which is the best indicator. In terms of its debt structure, OXSQ still lags behind industry leaders like Ares Capital (ARCC).

Conclusion

OXSQG has a currently high yield to maturity and is trading below par, making it attractive. The company has no credit rating, but has solid debt coverage and almost no problem loans. It is suitable for investors looking for higher yields with moderate risk.
